title: Command Cooldown
description: Trigger for when a Command is on Cooldown
summary: Check out the Commands Guide for details on configuring your Commands

parameters.name: Commands
parameters.description:
Select the command to monitor for cooldowns

::tip
You can quickly register a new command by clicking the `Create Command` button!

variables.description: The command that was used

variables.description: The id of the command

variables.description: How much seconds are left for the cooldown, and the maximum of the global and user cooldown

variables.name: userCooldownLeft
variables.description: How much seconds are left for the user cooldown

title: Command Triggered
description: Trigger for when a Command is Triggered
summary:
Check out the Commands Guide for details on configuring your Commands

Parameters

Select a command from the Commands tab.

variables.name: command
variables.description: The command that was used

variables.name: commandId
variables.description: The ID of the command

variables.name: commandName
variables.description: The name of the command

variables.name: commandSource
variables.description: The command source

variables.name: commandType
variables.description: The type of the command

variables.name: msgId
variables.description: The unique message id for this message

variables.name: rawInput
variables.description: The message entered, if the command was a Starts With, this will be removed

variables.name: rawInputEscaped
variables.description: The message escaped

variables.name: rawInputUrlEncoded
variables.description: The message URL encoded

variables.name: input#
variables.description: The `#` word of the message entered, spaces are delimiters and variable names are 0 indexed, so `input0` would give the first word, `input1` would give the second, and so on

variables.name: inputEscaped#
variables.description: The indexed word escaped

variables.name: inputUrlEncoded#
variables.description: The indexed word URL encoded

variables.name: counter
variables.description: A running total of how many times a command has been run since application launch

variables.name: userCounter
variables.description: A running total of how many times the command has been run by current user since application launch

variables.name: match.count
variables.description:
Only when the command mode = "Regex"

The number of match groups (named or unnamed) in the regex.

Note that this is always 1 more than the number of parentheses groups, as the entire match is implicitly group 0.

variables.name: match[<index>]
variables.description:
Only when the command mode = "Regex"

The text matching each indexed capture group.

Index 0 is the entire matching text, and the first parenthesized group is 1.

variables.name: <groupName>
variables.description:
Only when the command mode = "Regex"

When using named capture groups (e.g, `(?'points'\d+)` or `(?<points>\d+)` ), each named capture group becomes an argument containing the text of the match.

commonVariables.name: isReply
commonVariables.description: Is this message a reply?

commonVariables.name: reply.msgId
commonVariables.description: The message id of the reply

commonVariables.name: reply.userId
commonVariables.description: The user id from the user who sent the reply

commonVariables.name: reply.userLogin
commonVariables.description: The user login from the user who sent the reply

commonVariables.name: reply.userName
commonVariables.description: The user name from the user who sent the reply

commonVariables.name: reply.msgBody
commonVariables.description: The text content of the reply, **Spaces are replaced by `\s`**

commonVariables.name: reply.threadMsgId
commonVariables.description: The message id of the thread

commonVariables.name: reply.threadUserLogin
commonVariables.description: The user login name of the thread

title: Global Variable Created
description: Triggered when a global variable has been created

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of a specific global variable

Leave empty to trigger on **any** global variable.

parameters.name: Persisted
parameters.description:
- `Either` - Trigger on **both** persisted and non-persisted variables
- `Yes` - Trigger only on **persisted** variables
- `No` - Trigger only on **non-persisted** variables

title: Global Variable Deleted
description: Triggered when a global variable has been deleted

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of a specific global variable

Leave empty to trigger on **any** global variable.

parameters.name: Persisted
parameters.description:
- `Either` - Trigger on **both** persisted and non-persisted variables
- `Yes` - Trigger only on **persisted** variables
- `No` - Trigger only on **non-persisted** variables

title: Global Variable Updated
description: Triggered when a global variable has been updated

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of a specific global variable

Leave empty to trigger on **any** global variable.

parameters.name: Persisted
parameters.description:
- `Either` - Trigger on **both** persisted and non-persisted variables
- `Yes` - Trigger only on **persisted** variables
- `No` - Trigger only on **non-persisted** variables

variables.name: global.name
variables.description: The name of the global variable

variables.name: global.newValue
variables.description: The new value of the global variable

variables.name: global.oldValue
variables.description: The previous value of the global variable

variables.name: global.persisted
variables.description: Is this global variable persisted?

variables.name: global.lastWrite
variables.description: The timestamp of the last write

title: User Global Variable Updated
description: Triggered when a user global variable has been updated

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of a specific user global variable

Leave empty to trigger on **any** user global variable.

parameters.name: Persisted
parameters.description:
- `Either` - Trigger on **both** persisted and non-persisted variables
- `Yes` - Trigger only on **persisted** variables
- `No` - Trigger only on **non-persisted** variables

parameters.name: User Login
parameters.description:
Enter the username of a specific user

Leave empty to trigger on **any** username

parameters.name: Platform
parameters.description:
- `Any` - Trigger on variable updates for **all** platforms
- `Twitch` - Only trigger on **Twitch** user variable updates
- `VStream` - Only trigger on **VStream** user variable updates
- `YouTube` - Only trigger on **YouTube** user variable updates

variables.name: global.name
variables.description: The name of the global variable

variables.name: global.newValue
variables.description: The new value of the global variable

variables.name: global.oldValue
variables.description: The previous value of the global variable

variables.name: global.persisted
variables.description: Is this global variable persisted?

variables.name: global.lastWrite
variables.description: The timestamp of the last write

variables.name: global.userId
variables.description: The unique platform ID of the user

variables.name: global.userType
variables.description:
The platform of the user:
`twitch`, `youtube`, `kick`

variables.name: global.userLogin
variables.description: The username of the user

variables.name: global.userName
variables.description: The disply name of the user

Check out the MIDI Configuration Guide for details on configuring your MIDI devices

title: Process Started
description: Trigger for when a Process is Started
summary:
Details
Using this trigger requires you to have the Process Watcher enabled. This can be done under Services -> Process Watcher

Parameters

Input the name of the process

variables.name: name
variables.description: Name of the process watched for.

variables.name: executable
variables.description: Name of the process executable.

variables.name: path
variables.description: Path to the directory of the process.

variables.name: fullPath
variables.description: Exact path of the process executable.

variables.name: processId
variables.description: ID used in Windows for the process.

variables.name: parentProcessId
variables.description: ID of the program that launched the process.

title: Process Stopped
description: Trigger for when a Process is Stopped
summary:
Details
Using this trigger requires you to have the Process Watcher enabled. This can be done under Services -> Process Watcher

Parameters

Input the name of the process

variables.name: name
variables.description: Name of the process watched for.

variables.name: executable
variables.description: Name of the process executable.

variables.name: path
variables.description: Path to the directory of the process.

variables.name: fullPath
variables.description: Exact path of the process executable.

variables.name: processId
variables.description: ID used in Windows for the process.

variables.name: parentProcessId
variables.description: ID of the program that launched the process.

title: Custom Code Event
description: Trigger for a Custom Code Event
summary:
Details
This event can **only** be triggered with the CPH.TriggerCodeEvent C# Method. You can use two methods. With the first method you can use the variables of the current action. With the second method you can specify a dictionary of variables for the trigger.

When registering a trigger your trigger will end up in the Custom folder under triggers. You can nest the trigger in multiple folders by using the catergories parameter. To give the trigger a name use the triggerName parameter. Use the eventName parameter for a name only used in C# for triggering the method.

Variables
Variables are dependent on the custom event.

title: Custom
description: Trigger for a Custom Event
summary:
Details
This event can **only** be triggered with the CPH.TriggerEvent C# Method.

Variables
Variables are dependent on the custom event.

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Input the event name of the custom event

- Leaving this blank is considered a catch-all and will trigger on **any** custom event

title: Coin Exchange
description: When a CrowdControl user exchanges for CrowdControl's Coin currency

parameters.name: Coin Type
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Free`
- `Paid`

parameters.name: Exchange Source
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Paypal`
- `Stripe`
- `Tiltify`
- `Twitch Bits`/`Twitch Channel Points`/`Twitch Sub`

parameters.name: Amount
parameters.description:
Filter events between a specified `Min` and `Max`

`Min` and `Max` are **inclusive** of the entered values

- `Any Value` - Leave both min and max empty to accept any value
- `Exact Value` - Enter only a `Max` to trigger on a specific value
- `Greater Than` - Enter only a `Min` to trigger on any value greater than the specified value
- `Range` - Enter a min and a max to trigger within a desired range

variables.name: coinType

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Immediate`
- `Timed`

parameters.name: Price
parameters.description:
Filter events between a specified `Min` and `Max`

`Min` and `Max` are **inclusive** of the entered values

- `Any Value` - Leave both min and max empty to accept any value
- `Exact Value` - Enter only a `Max` to trigger on a specific value
- `Greater Than` - Enter only a `Min` to trigger on any value greater than the specified value
- `Range` - Enter a min and a max to trigger within a desired range

title: Remote Instance Trigger
description: Triggers when a remote Streamer.bot instance trigger gets sent
summary:
This trigger is executed by the Send Remote Trigger sub-action, sent from another instance.

Additional variables will be populated for all arguments configured in the sending sub-action

parameters.name: Instance
parameters.description:
Select the instance to send this trigger to

- `Any` will trigger *all* connected instances

::tip{to=/guide/integrations/streamerbot}
All instances must have the [Streamer.bot Website Integration](/guide/integrations/streamerbot) configured and enabled
::

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Name of the event to trigger on

- Leave blank to trigger on **any** incoming events
- To trigger on a specific event, enter the same name configured in the [Send Remote Trigger](/api/triggers/integrations/streamerbot-remote/remote-instance-trigger) sub-action on another instance

variables.description: The instance ID

variables.name: instanceName
variables.description: Name of the instance

variables.name: instanceVersion
variables.description: Streamer.bot version of the instance

variables.name: instanceOs
variables.description: The operation system of the instance

title: First Words
description: Trigger for a Kick First Words
summary:
Details
This triggers when someone sends their first message of the stream.

The exact reset time is set in Platforms -> Kick -> First Words and is 12h by default. That means that Streamer.bot must have been closed for 12 hours in order to reset the First Words trigger.

If you want to reset the First Words trigger, you can do so by going to Platforms -> Kick -> First Words and clicking the Reset button. This will reset the First Words trigger and allow it to be triggered again.

variables.name: firstMessage
variables.description: indicator whether the user's message was the first message **ever** in the channel

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for the Kick present viewers event
summary:
Notes
The Kick present viewer acts more like a chat activity tracker. There is no "Live-Update" option for Kick as the API does not provide the viewer list.

Under the Platforms > Kick > Settings, you will find the options for the Present Viewers Trigger.

The slider behaves as a threshold. The timer runs every minute, and checks the current time minus the user's last active time, if this is less then the threshold, they are marked as present, otherwise they are marked as not present.  The trigger will be executed every minute.

title: Ad Mid-Roll
description: Trigger for a Twitch Ad Mid-Roll
summary:
**Removed in Streamer.bot v1.0.0**
Twitch has deprecated the PubSub API and all triggers relying on it have been removed. It is **not recommended** to rely on this trigger. - This trigger utilizes an **undocumented and unsupported** Twitch PubSub event which could be removed, without warning, in the future. title: Automatic Reward Redemption
description: Trigger for when default channel point rewards or Power-ups have been used
summary:
Details
The Automatic Reward Redemptions work for both **default rewards** as well as the newly introduced **Power-ups**.

Default Rewards
The default rewards are those made by Twitch like "Highlight My Message". You can either choose to enable or disable them, but you cannot delete and only restrictively edit them.

!Default Rewards

parameters.name: Reward
parameters.description: Select any or a specific automatic channel reward type.

variables.name: rewardType
variables.description:
Type of the reward

Possible values: `send_highlighted_message`, `random_sub_emote_unlock`, `chosen_sub_emote_unlock`, `chosen_modified_sub_emote_unlock`, `single_message_bypass_sub_mode`, `message_effect`, `gigantify_an_emote`, `celebration`

variables.description: the input message of the redeeming user (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: gigantifiedEmoteId
variables.description: the ID of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.description: the name of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

title: Bot Whispers
description: Trigger for a Twitch Bot Whisper
summary:
Notes
The user sending the whisper must have a **verified phone number** (see the **Phone Number setting** in your **Security and Privacy settings**).

The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies, it will still indicate success even if the message is dropped.

Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of **40 unique recipients per day**. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

title: First Words
description: Trigger for a Twitch First Words
summary:
This triggers when someone sends their first message of the stream.

The exact reset time is set in Platforms -> Twitch -> Settings and is 12h by default. That means that StreamerBot must have been closed for 12 hours in order to reset the First Words trigger.

You can create a new action, then create a  Settings -> Reset First Words subaction and assign a Twitch -> Channel -> Stream Online trigger. This will automatically reset the First Words trigger once you go live without any time requirement.

parameters.name: User Name
parameters.description: If you want to filter on a user name, you can enter a user name in this field

parameters.name: Is UserId
parameters.description: Check this field if the text in the `User Name` field is a user id

variables.name: firstMessage
variables.description: indicator whether the user's message was the first message **ever** in the channel

title: Whispers
description: Trigger for a Twitch Whisper
summary:
Notes
The user sending the whisper must have a **verified phone number** (see the **Phone Number setting** in your **Security and Privacy settings**).

The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies, it will still indicate success even if the message is dropped.

Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of **40 unique recipients per day**. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for the Twitch present viewers event
summary:
Details
This triggers every 1-10 minutes, default every 5 minutes.

Notes
The present viewer tick will always happen, but you have the ability to have it "live update" from Twitch, or artificially mark someone as present.

Under the Platforms > Twitch > Settings, in the Present Viewers section, there are 2 settings, a Live Update check box, and a slider bar.

Choose on how much months gifted to trigger the gift sub

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for YouTube viewer changes
summary:
Notes
The YouTube present viewer acts more like a chat activity tracker. There is no "Live-Update" option for YouTube as the API does not provide the viewer list.

Under the Platforms > YouTube > Settings, you will find the options for the Present Viewers Trigger.

The slider behaves as a threshold. The timer runs every minute, and checks the current time minus the user's last active time, if this is less then the threshold, they are marked as present, otherwise they are marked as not present.  The trigger will be executed every minute.
